articles.title: Claudia Winkleman's New TV Show Gets Mixed Reviews
articles.summary: Claudia Winkleman started a new talk show on BBC One. She is a famous TV host from shows like The Traitors. On her first show, she talked to guests like Jeff Goldblum, Vanessa Williams, Jennifer Saunders, and Tom Allen. Critics had different opinions about the show. Some liked it and gave it five stars, saying it was fun and friendly. Others did not like it as much, giving it two or three stars and calling it boring or messy. The show is part of Claudia's big career on TV, and people are watching to see if it gets better over time. The guests talked about their new projects, like music, movies, and books.

articles.title: Help for Families with High Heating Oil Bills
articles.summary: Chancellor Rachel Reeves is planning to help families who are paying more for heating oil. Heating oil is used in many homes, especially in rural areas, to keep warm and have hot water. The cost of heating oil has gone up because of a war in Iran involving the US and Israel. This makes it hard for people to afford their bills. The government knows people are worried and wants to offer support soon. About 1.7 million homes in England and Wales use heating oil, and prices can change a lot. The chancellor says she will find money to help those who need it most. This support is not for gas or electricity bills, which have different rules. The government is working on plans to make sure families can stay warm without spending too much money.

articles.title: Girls Racing Fast Cars to Change Formula 1
articles.summary: This article is about girls who love to race cars and want to be part of Formula 1, a big racing sport. Rachel Robertson is an 18-year-old from England who drives fast race cars in a special championship called F1 Academy, which is only for women. She started racing go-karts when she was 14 and often raced against boys who didn't think girls should race. They would try to push her off the track or not talk to her when she won. Another driver, Esmee Kosterman from the Netherlands, also faced boys who said racing wasn't for girls. Both girls are working hard to show that girls can be great racers and hope to race in Formula 1 one day. They are breaking barriers in a sport that has mostly been for men and rich people.

articles.title: A Leader Steps Down at a Famous Arts Center
articles.summary: Ric Grenell is leaving his job as president of the Kennedy Center, a big performing arts place in Washington, D.C. President Trump announced this on social media. Grenell helped make changes at the center, like adding Trump's name to it. Many artists, such as those from the musical "Hamilton," stopped performing there because of these changes. The Kennedy Center is known for honoring famous artists and bringing people together, but it became more connected to Trump's ideas. Now, Matt Floca will take over as president. The center will close soon for a two-year renovation to make it better. Grenell has worked for Trump in other jobs, like being an ambassador.

articles.title: Georgia Senators Ask Questions About a Trump Case
articles.summary: In Georgia, state senators asked Nathan Wade, a former prosecutor, about his work on a case involving former President Donald Trump and the 2020 election. The senators wanted to know about talks his team had with federal investigators, but Wade said he couldn't remember many details. This happened at a hearing where a committee was looking into how the case was handled. The case was about Trump and others trying to change election results in Georgia. Earlier, the prosecutor leading the case, Fani Willis, was removed because she had a relationship with Wade, which some thought was a problem. Wade defended the team's work, saying they did it on their own without outside help. The senators focused on billing records and meetings, but Wade kept saying he didn't recall specifics.

articles.title: Attack on a Jewish Place in Michigan Shows More Hate Crimes
articles.summary: In Michigan, a man drove a truck into a synagogue, which is a place where Jewish people pray and learn. The FBI says this was a targeted act of violence against the Jewish community. The attacker had fireworks and gasoline in his truck, but he died from a self-inflicted gunshot wound. This event has started a bigger talk about antisemitism, which is hate against Jewish people, in the United States. Leaders like the governor of Michigan say it was plain hate. People are now discussing how to stop such attacks and protect communities.
